contract.receipt: side effect — one receipt line per successful review

modeCatalog.purpose: a second claimant of the SAME contract invocation
a reference past the end of the contract array
whenToUse: never — the index does not resolve

modeCatalog.purpose: a reference into a contract field that carries no invocation descriptors
whenToUse: never — receipt is not an invocation array

modeCatalog.purpose: a contract-backed primary that references nothing
whenToUse: never — a contract-backed entry composes by reference

modeCatalog.purpose: a contract-backed entry ALSO carrying a literal descriptor
whenToUse: never — the literal would duplicate the contract

modeCatalog.purpose: a contract-FREE primary with no literal descriptor
whenToUse: never — nothing renders an invocation for it

modeCatalog.purpose: an invocationRefs element that is not a {contractField, index} object
whenToUse: never — a bare string is not a reference

modeCatalog.purpose: a reference whose index is not a non-negative integer
whenToUse: never — the index must address a real array slot

modeCatalog.purpose: a primary claiming a continuation descriptor
whenToUse: never — a primary drives a mode, it does not resume one

modeCatalog.purpose: a continuation claiming a primary drive form
whenToUse: never — a continuation resumes, it does not drive

modeCatalog.purpose: a continuation on a contract-free role, carrying a literal descriptor
whenToUse: never — the literal-descriptor exception covers primaries and env-hooks only
